prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: remake the Children of the Prophecy Aevum Guardian so it preserves the approved guardian identity but visually belongs to the current Mirage Seer and Memory Keeper squad family
Subject: the same frontline ascetic warrior with the broad conical hat, wrapped face, long polearm or rifle-staff, and grounded guardian posture, but rendered as the martial frontline member of this squad with clearer cloth layering, stronger silhouette readability, and the same desert-prophetic lineage as the approved Mirage Seer and Memory Keeper
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with painterly pixel-art-inspired rendering, larger readable forms, restrained texture, and clean downscaling at small tactical size
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, grounded stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, readable when rendered around 104 px tall on a tactics board
Lighting/mood: solemn dust-filtered dusk light with controlled contrast and only the faintest sacred-tech accent; spiritual but disciplined, not flashy
Color palette: sun-bleached ivory cloth, ash beige, muted rust red, worn brown leather, restrained pale gold and faint ghostly teal accents used sparingly
Constraints: preserve the recognizable Aevum Guardian identity from the approved runtime asset, especially the broad hat silhouette, wrapped face, and polearm profile; keep this unit clearly more martial and frontline-oriented than Mirage Seer and Memory Keeper; align the rendering language and fabric treatment with the approved Children squad; exactly one subject; no floor; no scenery; no text; no extra subjects; no oversized empty padding
Avoid: bare-chested fighter read, neon magic, anime rendering, glossy sci-fi materials, giant VFX, floating pose, cinematic background, noisy micro-detail, ornate fantasy armor
